question:
Will Cherif Younousse & Ahmed Tijan win the 2025 Beach Volleyball World Championships (Men’s Tournament)?

Description: The 2025 Beach Volleyball World Championships (Men’s) are scheduled for 14–23 November 2025.

This polymarket will resolve to the team that wins the 2025 Beach Volleyball World Championships (Men’s Tournament).

The market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed pair wins the 2025 Beach Volleyball World Championships (Men’s Tournament). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this pair to win the 2025 Beach Volleyball World Championships (Men’s Tournament) per the official rules, this market will resolve to “No”.

If, for any reason, the winners of the event are not officially determined within 30 days after the scheduled final, this market will be settled as “Other.”

The primary resolution source will be official information from the FIVB. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

evidences.title: Qatar’s Cherif & Ahmed get back to number two in the FIVB Beach Volleyball World Rankings for the first time in over three tears
evidences.content: Beach Pro Tour Beach Pro Tour # Cherif & Ahmed number two in World Rankings for the first time in over three years Nuss & Brasher climb back up to number four Published 04:17, 15 Oct 2025 Share on Whatsapp Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Copy link Published 04:17, 15 Oct 2025 Cherif/Ahmed (QAT) vs. Evandro/Arthur Lanci (BRA) - Final 1st Place #67112158 Qatar’s Cherif & Ahmed take a selfie in celebration of their Newport Beach gold [...] THE BEST OF CHERIF/AHMED IN NEWPORT BEACH 🇺🇸! 🥇 They have now 4️⃣ golds on the Beach Pro Tour this season: 1 Challenge gold in Xiamen and 3 Elite golds (Gstaad, Rio and Newport Beach)! 🏆 ​📺 Relive it on VBTV! ​ 🔜 Next stop: Cape Town 🇿🇦 | Oct 22-26 ​ 🏐 #BeachProTourNewport pic.twitter.com/LmBYiTzJX9 Newport Beach Elite runners-up Evandro Oliveira & Arthur Mariano Lanci of Brazil also gained two spots in the ranking, climbing from number seven to number five after netting 500 of the 1100 points that came with their silver. They are now 320 points below fourth-ranked reigning Olympic champions David Ahman & Jonatan Hellvig of Sweden, who are another 100 points below third-ranked Stefan Boermans & Yorick de Groot of the Netherlands. evidences.title: 2025 FIVB WCHs Specific Competition Regulations V1_Website_Final_18Jul2025.pdf
evidences.content: Preliminary phase: Pool Play 48 teams playing in (12) pools of four (4) teams each played in standard Round Robin system . A drawing of lots will be held on 9 October 2025 in Adelaide to establish the seeding and distribution into pools. Every team will play the three other teams in their pool. The ranking of teams within their pools will be determined by the total number of match points, with the following points awarded per match to each team: Rules for pool ranking. • Winner earns 2 match points. Loser earns 1 match point . Match won 2 points Match lost 1 point 12 • For all matches the final team ranking will be determined in accordance with the results of the matches (won or lost). For each match won the winning team will earn 2 match points, for each [...] October in Adelaide to establish the distribution of teams into pools. All teams will be listed at the drawing of lots , seeded as per the FIVB WCH Entry Points calculated on 6th October 2025 . For the sake of clarity, the seeding points are based on the sum of ranking points accumulated from the best six (6) finishes over the period of 1 st March to 5th October 2025 . The best host country team ( Australia ) will be seed...

evidences.title: Cherif and Ahmed continue unstoppable and claim Volleyball World Beach Pro Tour Newport Beach Elite title
evidences.content: The hottest men’s team in international beach volleyball right now, they made it to the top of the podium in California, flanked by two South American duos, as Brazilians Evandro Gonçalves and Arthur Lanci finished second and the Chilean Grimalt cousins Marco and Esteban ranked third in the tenth Elite event of the 2025 season. A post shared by Beach Volleyball World (@beachvolleyballworld) The 2025 season has been a different one for Cherif and Ahmed, who typically compete in almost every Elite event there is, but this year were limited to only six appearances on the Beach Pro Tour because of injuries. [...] The bronze medalists of the Tokyo 2020 Olympics, have, however, made the most of every opportunity they had to compete in 2025, winning four golds, with victories in the Elite events held in Gstaad, Rio de Janeiro and Newport Beach and the Xiamen Challenge. Disregarding the three forfeit defeats they were forced to accept at the Montréal Elite after Cherif got injured, the two have won 17 of their last 19 matches, including the last 11. “Winning here feels amazing,” Ahmed commented. “My wife is from the United States, and I am very happy to be here and win the gold medal. I...

evidences.title: Empowered Qatar duo chases breakthrough World Championship medal – FIVB
evidences.content: Qatar’s celebrated beach volleyball duo Cherif Younousse & Ahmed Tijan have already made history for their nation. Their bronze medal at the Olympic Games Tokyo 2020 was a landmark achievement, marking the first Olympic beach volleyball medal ever won by an Asian team. Four years later, they confirmed their place among the world’s elite with a fourth-place finish in Paris 2024, further establishing their reputation as one of the most consistent and dynamic pairs on the world stage. [...] This year, Cherif & Ahmed have maintained their winning form on the Beach Pro Tour, capturing gold medals at some of the most competitive stops. Their victories in Gstaad, Rio de Janeiro and Newport Beach showcased their trademark mix of power, control and composure, with Cherif earning the Best Blocker award in the United States event. Each result has reinforced their position among the world’s leading pairs and underlined their readiness for the season’s biggest stage in Adelaide. Ahmed Tijan sets the ball during a match at the Beach Pro Tour Elite16 Rio. [...] Their group stage schedule includes matches against Cuba’s Damian Gomez & Eblis Veranes on 15 November, Switzerland’s Yves Haussener &...

evidences.title: Competition Format for the 2025 FIVB Beach Volleyball World Championships
evidences.content: The total number of matches to be played is 216 (108 per gender). The 2025 FIVB Beach Volleyball World Championships 2025 consist of a pool phase and a single elimination phase. Pool Phase In each gender, the 48 participating teams are split into 12 single round-robin pools of four. The pool winners, the pool runners-up and the best four of the third-placed teams (first ranked by match points, then by set ratio, then by rally point ratio and lastly by the tournament seeding established at the general technical meeting), advance straight to the sixteenthfinals. The remaining eight third-placed teams in the pools advance to the first knockout round, dubbed “the lucky loser round”. Single Elimination Phase [...] Beach Volleyball World Championships Adelaide 2025 "Beach Volleyball World Championships Adelaide 2025") Beach Volleyball World Championships Adelaide 2025 ## Advertising Beach Volleyball World Championships Adelaide 2025 Beach Volleyball World Championships Adelaide 2025 "Beach Volleyball World Championships Adelaide 2025") # Beach Volleyball World Championships Adelaide 2025 - Competition. # COMPETITION FORMAT: 2025 FIVB Beach Volleyball World Championships The 15th editi...

evidences.title: Qatar duo chases breakthrough World Championship medal
evidences.content: Their group stage schedule includes matches against Cuba’s Damian Gomez & Eblis Veranes on 15 November, Switzerland’s Yves Haussener & Julian Friedli on 16 November and Austria’s Timo Hammarberg & Tim Berger on 17 November. The pair enter the competition aiming to capture a World Championship medal, the major title missing from their record.

evidences.title: Qatar’s Cherif Younousse & Ahmed Tijan win the Rio de Janeiro Elite for their third Volleyball World Beach Pro Tour gold of the year
evidences.content: Cherif/Ahmed vs. Alayo/Diaz - Match Highlights, 09/28/2025 “For me, this place has always been a great place to remember, because this is where I played my first Olympics and it was amazing here in Copacabana. The fans make us feel at home here,” Cherif told VBTV after the final. “It was a great fight against Cuba tonight, but in the end, it was beach volleyball that won.” “This is unbelievable! I don’t know how we did it, but we had a great performance,” Ahmed added. “Being far away from the family is not easy, but I would like to thank my family back home for being patient. I have a new-born baby waiting for me back at home and I am so happy that I can bring the gold medal to her.” [...] Elite16 - Rio de Janeiro, BRA - 2025 Elite16 - Rio de Janeiro, BRA - 2025 ## Advertising Elite16 - Rio de Janeiro, BRA - 2025 Beach Pro Tour 2025 Home Elite16 - Rio de Janeiro, BRA - 2025 # Elite16 - Rio de Janeiro, BRA - 2025 - Beach Pro Tour 2025 season - News # Qatar’s Cherif & Ahmed win Rio for third Beach Pro Tour gold of the year Cuba’s Diaz & Alayo and Netherlands’ Boermans & De Groot complete the podium in Brazil Published 09:46, 28 Sept 2025 Share on Whatsapp Share on Facebook Share o...

In case of two or more hosts, those are placed at the head of pools based on their position in the respective World Volleyball Ranking (Senior or Age Group), i.e. top ranked host at the head of pool [...] the Competition Regulations. 7.1.1 When there are a maximum of four (4) teams in each preliminary group, the teams will be directly seeded in the first position of each preliminary group based upon the respective World Volleyball Ranking (Senior or Age Group) and adopting the serpentine system. evidences.title: Titanic clashes coming up in Ostrava
evidences.content: The teams ranked second, fourth and seventh in the world are set to deliver some titanic clashes in Pool B. Fourth-ranked Swedes David Ahman & Jonatan Hellvig headline the pool as the second-seeded duo in the Ostrava main draw. The 21-year-old reigning European champions will be looking for their seventh Beach Pro Tour podium, but will have to go against the world’s number two team, Alexander Brouwer & Robert Meeuwsen of the Netherlands, in their first match of the tournament. The 10th-seeded Dutchmen have previously collected four medals on the Tour, including an Ostrava 2022 bronze, but are still hungry for their first gold. Also aiming for their fifth Beach Pro Tour podium, Tokyo 2020 Olympic bronze medallists and former World Ranking leaders Cherif Younousse & Ahmed Tijan of Qatar are [...] The teams ranked second, fourth and seventh in the world are set to deliver some titanic clashes in Pool B. Fourth-ranked Swedes David Ahman & Jonatan Hellvig headline the pool as the second-seeded duo in the Ostrava main draw. The 21-year-old reigning European champions will be looking for their seventh Beach Pro Tour podium, but will have to go against the world’s number two team, Alexa...

evidences.title: Women’s Beach Volleyball World Ranking | Volleyball World
evidences.content: ### How Are the Rankings Calculated? The rankings are based on a points system that accumulates based on players' performances in various FIVB-sanctioned tournaments. Each tournament is assigned a specific point value, with larger, more prestigious tournaments offering more points. For example, winning a World Championship event yields more points than a lower-tier tournament. The more consistently a team or player performs at a high level, the more points they accumulate, helping them rise in the rankings. This system ensures that the rankings reflect not only one-time successes but sustained excellence over time. ### Key Tournaments Impacting the Rankings [...] ### How Are the Rankings Calculated? The rankings are based on a points system that accumulates based on players' performances in various FIVB-sanctioned tournaments. Each tournament is assigned a specific point value, with larger, more prestigious tournaments offering more points. For example, winning a World Championship event yields more points than a lower-tier tournament. The more consistently a team or player performs at a high level, the more points they accumulate, helping them rise in the rankings. This system...
